cluster_summaries.label: Pay & Benefits
cluster_summaries.summary: Employees frequently express satisfaction with their financial compensation, benefits, and the availability of corporate perks.

cluster_summaries.label: Flexibility & Care
cluster_summaries.summary: This area highlights the importance of work-life balance, with many staff members appreciating the collegiate and flexible environment.

cluster_summaries.label: Growth & Recognition
cluster_summaries.summary: Employees value opportunities for ownership and career advancement, though these experiences are not universal across all teams.

cluster_summaries.label: Culture & Belonging
cluster_summaries.summary: Supportive team environments and community engagement programs contribute to a sense of belonging for many employees.

cluster_summaries.label: Safety & Fairness
cluster_summaries.summary: When the culture is perceived as inclusive and well-managed, employees report feeling secure and valued in their roles.

cluster_summaries.label: Burnout & Workload
cluster_summaries.summary: A subset of the workforce reports exhaustion due to long hours and high-pressure demands typical of banking roles.

cluster_summaries.label: Work-Life Pressure
cluster_summaries.summary: Inflexible schedules and demanding workloads create significant friction for employees attempting to balance professional and personal life.

cluster_summaries.label: Unsafe or Unfair Culture
cluster_summaries.summary: Reports of toxic environments, internal politics, and favoritism negatively impact the sense of safety and trust among staff.

cluster_summaries.label: Stagnation
cluster_summaries.summary: Lack of clear career progression and poor management communication are cited as primary drivers of frustration.

cluster_summaries.label: Disconnected Culture
cluster_summaries.summary: Some employees feel isolated or disconnected from the broader organization due to uneven workload distribution and internal silos.
